summary: Iteration 16 — Cross-page KPI equality verification (Vivo BI dashboard). Tested Overview, Locations, Products, Footfall and CEO Report against the shared useKpis / useKpisLMLY hook. PRIMARY ASK (numeric equality across pages) PASSES for both the default 'Today' range and a custom MTD range (2026-04-01 → 2026-04-23). Cache-busting `_t` query param is appended to 100% of /api/kpis GET requests. CEO Report scorecard AND the Country table TOTAL row agree byte-for-byte with Overview's Total Sales / Net Sales / Orders / Units. No P0/P1 functional issues found; one usability nit around preset-click → other-page propagation when the tester uses hard navigation.

issues: Recharts ResponsiveContainer still emits `The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0` warnings — pre-existing from iteration 15, low priority.

test_results_detail.console_errors: None — only pre-existing Recharts width/height warnings (iteration 15 already flagged).
test_results_detail.spa_nav_vs_hard_nav: Filter context (date range etc.) is preserved only across SPA (React Router <Link>) navigation. Using `page.goto(/locations)` triggers a fresh SPA boot and resets applied filters to the default preset (Today). This is expected React Context behaviour and matches real user interaction (they click nav links).

action_items: (Optional / LOW) In Filters context, persist the date range + compareMode to localStorage or URL search params so the filter survives page refresh and direct URL hits (currently it resets to 'Today' on reload).
(LOW) Fix Recharts ResponsiveContainer width(-1) warnings on customer-frequency-chart and any other charts lacking explicit min dimensions — still pending from iteration 15.
critical_code_review_comments: useKpis caches by (date_from, date_to, country, channel, dataVersion) — good. But `fetchKpis` sends _v as `params._v` via axios.get, AND the global interceptor adds `_t`. Backend ignores _v and _t, so it is effectively just a cache-key. Works, but slightly confusing; consider renaming the interceptor-level param to `cb` and stripping `_v` from the URL.

filters.jsx defaults to preset 'today' on every mount; since FiltersProvider lives at app root it survives SPA nav, but a full reload wipes it. Persisting to localStorage/sessionStorage would make the 'same numbers everywhere' story also hold across browser refreshes.

When the user clicks the 'This Month' preset chip, setPreset calls setPresetKey then setDateFrom/setDateTo synchronously — two renders, two downstream useEffect runs, two /kpis calls in quick succession. Not a bug (cache deduplicates), but worth batching via useReducer for tidier React profiler traces.
useKpis.js line 54 mutates `kpiCache.get(key).data = r.data` directly (works but bypasses Map.set contract). Prefer `kpiCache.set(key, { ...entry, data: r.data })` for clarity and immutability.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the primary ask — cross-page KPI equality verified for two different date ranges; cache-busting verified 100%.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Cross-page KPI equality works end-to-end when the user navigates via SPA links. Always use `await page.click('a:has-text("Locations")')` style, NOT `page.goto(...)`, when validating shared-filter behaviour — `page.goto` triggers a full SPA reboot and resets the in-memory filter context, producing false P0s. Cache-bust `_t` is applied globally by the axios request interceptor in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js, so every GET (not just /kpis) gets it — verified 16/16 requests carry _t=.
rca_of_the_issue: No live bug. Initial test run used `page.goto(/locations)` after changing the date on Overview; this full-page reload resets the FiltersProvider context to its default 'today' preset, causing Locations/Products/Footfall/CEO to show the Today numbers while Overview still had MTD numbers in-memory. Retested with SPA click navigation — all five pages then show identical KPIs for the same filter state (both Today and 1–23 Apr ranges). Root cause of the initial false-positive: React Context state is not persisted. Mitigation (nice-to-have): persist `dateFrom/dateTo/compareMode/countries/channels` to localStorage or URL params in filters.jsx so even full reloads keep the numbers in sync.
